query:
thoughts?  Confirmed — fully deterministic. Here's the breakdown:
                                        
  Tier 1 (isolated entries): No sort at all — order is whatever all_active comes back as (likely insertion
   order from SQLite).                                                                                    
                                                                                                          
  Tier 2 (non-isolated): sort_by(|a, b| b.created_at.cmp(&a.created_at)) — sorted created_at descending,  
  same order every tick.                                                                                  
                                                                                                          
  After tick 1 processes the ~65 isolated candidates and writes 6 edges, those 6 entries move out of      
  isolated_ids. But the rest of tier 1 (entries that had no-embedding and were skipped in Phase 4) stay in
   the isolated set — so tick 2 re-selects the same pool. The ones with embeddings were already fully     
  evaluated; the ones without embeddings are skipped again in Phase 4. Net result: 0 new pairs.

  The stuck state is structural, not a fluke:                                                             
   
  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐          
  │    What stays the same tick-to-tick    │                        Why                        │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤          
  │ Tier 2 source order                    │ created_at sort is stable                         │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Tier 1 re-selects no-embedding entries │ They never leave isolated_ids                     │          
  ├────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤          
  │ HNSW neighbor expansion                │ Same embedding → same neighbors                   │          
  ├────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤          
  │ existing_supports_pairs pre-filter     │ 6 new edges excluded, but rest of pairs re-scored │
  └────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘          
                                               
  Practical fix options (in order of invasiveness):                                                       
                                               
  1. Shuffle tier 1 and tier 2 independently — use_thread_rng().shuffle() before take(max_sources).       
  Cheapest, doesn't require schema changes. Different candidates selected each tick.
  2. Exclude no-embedding sources at selection time — check vector_index.get_embedding(id).is_some()      
  during select_source_candidates. Would require passing the vector index in, but prevents permanently    
  stuck no-embedding entries from consuming tier 1 slots.
  3. Attempted-pairs bloom filter — persist (source, target) pairs tried this tick/session and skip       
  re-scoring. Heavier, but prevents re-scoring known-bad pairs even after shuffle redistributes

entries.title: Tick source-candidate selection with stable ordering stalls after first window is exhausted
Tick passes over full entry set: bound, shuffle, and embedding-filter source candidates independently of NLI pair cap
ADR-003: Bidirectional Contradicts Query Required for Correctness (col-030)
Promotion tick SELECT must JOIN entries for quarantine filter on both outer query and normalization subquery
ADR-003 crt-037: Directional Dedup for query_existing_informs_pairs
